claim: Least-to-Most Prompting was submitted in May 2022 and reports strong compositional generalization, including SCAN accuracy above 99% with few exemplars.
ReAct was submitted in October 2022 and evaluates interleaved reasoning/actions on HotpotQA, FEVER, ALFWorld, and WebShop, including large absolute gains on ALFWorld and WebShop.
Self-Ask introduces explicit follow-up question decomposition and shows that the structure can be combined with search for compositional questions.
HyDE uses hypothetical documents for zero-shot dense retrieval and reports gains across web search, QA, fact verification, and multilingual retrieval tasks.
Toolformer trains models to decide when and how to call APIs, covering tools such as calculator, QA, search, translation, and calendar.
Reflexion uses verbal feedback memory rather than weight updates and reports 91% pass@1 on HumanEval in the paper abstract.
Tree of Thoughts reports 74% success on Game of 24 compared with 4% for GPT-4 chain-of-thought in its experimental setup.
Voyager reports strong Minecraft lifelong-learning gains, including 3.3x more unique items, 2.3x longer travel, and up to 15.3x faster milestone unlocks.
ReWOO separates reasoning from observations and reports 5x token efficiency plus a 4% HotpotQA accuracy improvement.

AutoGen was submitted in August 2023 as a multi-agent conversation framework with empirical studies across several application domains, but the evidence is framework-level rather than a single isolated loop benchmark.
MetaGPT encodes SOPs into multi-agent workflows for software engineering; it is important for role/SOP orchestration but benchmark attribution is coupled to the framework.
Self-RAG adaptively retrieves and critiques generations, reporting improvements on open-domain QA, reasoning, fact verification, and long-form factuality/citation tasks.
Corrective RAG evaluates retrieval correction on four datasets covering short- and long-form generation and reports improved RAG performance.
CodeAct uses executable Python code as an action space and reports up to 20% higher success rate across API-Bank and a curated benchmark with 17 LLMs.
SWE-agent evaluates an agent-computer interface on SWE-bench and HumanEvalFix, reporting 12.5% and 87.7% pass@1 respectively at publication.
CRAG is a comprehensive RAG benchmark; the paper reports advanced LLMs at 34% or less and straightforward RAG around 44% accuracy.
LangGraph documentation positions it as an orchestration runtime with durable execution, streaming, human-in-the-loop, and persistence.

OpenAI announced the Agents SDK in March 2025 with orchestration primitives such as multi-agent workflows, handoffs, guardrails, and tracing; local fetch returned 403, so this is cross-checked via browser/search result and docs URL.
Onyx proposes programmable agent orchestration with run, spawn, typed state, sleep, checkpoint, budget and error semantics; public benchmark evidence is not established in the article.
